name: Deer Trail (PR12 Guadramil)
name_original: Trilho dos Cervídeos (PR 12) — Parque Natural de Montesinho
description: Hike the moderate 5.5-mile Deer Trail (PR12 Guadramil) in Montesinho Natural Park, enjoying wild landscapes and potential deer sightings.
region_intro: Hiking around Rio De Onor, Portugal, offers trails through the Montesinho Natural Park, characterized by rolling hills, traditional villages, and river valleys. The landscape features a mix of open countryside and forested areas, providing varied terrain for exploration. This region, part of the Trás-os-Montes e Alto Douro province, is known for its natural beauty and preserved rural character. Best hiking trails around Rio De Onor The most popular hiking route is The Portuguese Side of Rio de Onor (PR 11) — circular route in the Montesinho Natural Park, an easy 4.3 miles (6.9 km) trail that takes about 1 hour 52 minutes to complete. This circular path offers views of the traditional village and surrounding natural park. Another top favourite among local hikers is Deer Trail (PR12 Guadramil), a moderate 5.5 miles (8.8 km) path. This route winds through the natural park, offering a chance to experience the region's wildlife and diverse flora. Local hikers also love the Trail of the Poço Negro Waterfall — Parque Natural de Montesinho, an 8.2 miles (13.2 km) trail leading through the Parque Natural de Montesinho to a notable waterfall, often completed in about 3 hours 33 minutes. Hiking around Rio De Onor is defined by its rural landscapes, river valleys, and forested hills within the Montesinho Natural Park. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more moderate trails. The routes in Rio De Onor are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 90 hikers have used komoot to explore Rio De Onor's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Rio De Onor?
A: There are several hiking trails around Rio De Onor, with komoot listing 4 distinct routes. These trails offer a range of experiences within the Montesinho Natural Park.

Q: Are there easy hiking options for beginners or casual walkers?
A: Yes, Rio De Onor offers easy hiking options. An excellent choice is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2172563424/the-portuguese-side-of-rio-de-onor-pr-11-circular-route-in-the-montesinho-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Portuguese Side of Rio de Onor (PR 11)&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 4.3-mile (6.9 km) circular route that takes about 1 hour 52 minutes to complete, offering views of the traditional village and surrounding natural park.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see on the trails?
A: Hiking around Rio De Onor is characterized by rural landscapes, river valleys, and forested hills, all within the Montesinho Natural Park. You'll encounter a mix of open countryside and wooded areas, reflecting the region's natural beauty and preserved rural character.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Yes, circular routes are available. The popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2172563424/the-portuguese-side-of-rio-de-onor-pr-11-circular-route-in-the-montesinho-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Portuguese Side of Rio de Onor (PR 11)&lt;/a&gt; is a circular trail, allowing you to start and end in the same location without retracing your steps.

Q: What are some interesting natural sights or landmarks along the trails?
A: The trails around Rio De Onor offer various natural and cultural sights. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4945816" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Scenic landscape in Parque Natural de Montesinho&lt;/a&gt;, enjoy the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4954373" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Montesinho Hills&lt;/a&gt;, or discover the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5814010" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Weir and Boardwalk of the Rio Onor&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2165070574/trail-of-the-poco-negro-waterfall-parque-natural-de-montesinho" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail of the Poço Negro Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; leads to a notable waterfall within the natural park.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Rio De Onor?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 30 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, the preserved rural character, and the opportunity to experience the region's wildlife and flora within the Montesinho Natural Park.

Q: Are there any moderate trails for more experienced hikers?
A: Yes, there are several moderate trails for those seeking a bit more challenge. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2167070666/deer-trail-pr12-guadramil" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Deer Trail (PR12 Guadramil)&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 5.5-mile (8.8 km) path, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2165070574/trail-of-the-poco-negro-waterfall-parque-natural-de-montesinho" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail of the Poço Negro Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is an 8.2-mile (13.2 km) moderate route.

Q: Can I find trails that offer a chance to see wildlife?
A: The Montesinho Natural Park is known for its wildlife. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2167070666/deer-trail-pr12-guadramil" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Deer Trail (PR12 Guadramil)&lt;/a&gt;, in particular, winds through the natural park, offering a good chance to experience the region's diverse flora and potentially spot local wildlife.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike in Rio De Onor?
A: The Montesinho Natural Park, where Rio De Onor is located, offers beautiful scenery throughout the year. Spring and autumn generally provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes, while summer can be warm but ideal for exploring river valleys. Winter offers a different, often quieter, experience.

Q: Are there any traditional villages or settlements to explore near the hiking routes?
A: Absolutely. Rio de Onor itself is a traditional village, and its unique cross-border character is a highlight. You can also find other charming settlements nearby, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5720702" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montesinho Village&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6340020" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;França&lt;/a&gt;, which offer a glimpse into the region's preserved rural life.

Q: Are there any trails that lead to a waterfall?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2165070574/trail-of-the-poco-negro-waterfall-parque-natural-de-montesinho" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail of the Poço Negro Waterfall — Parque Natural de Montesinho&lt;/a&gt; is specifically designed to lead you to a notable waterfall within the natural park, offering a refreshing destination during your hike.
tips:
In this corner of rolling countryside, between peaceful valleys and gentle hills, there is a large population of fallow deer and stags.

During the roaring season (between September and October) the chances of seeing them are very high. Just get up early or wait until dusk, as these elegant creatures prefer the cool and stay away from the heat. With a little silence and luck, you will be able to witness one of nature's most magical spectacles.

The landscapes of Guadramil are a true wildlife sanctuary. Deer are the stars, of course, but they are far from the only charms of this landscape. Here, nature reveals itself in all its power, between imposing valleys and breathtaking horizons. Every bend in the trail can surprise you with an animal encounter or a simply unforgettable view.

Guadramil, lost among the mountains of the Montesinho Natural Park, is one of the most isolated villages in Portugal, so remote that not even a mobile phone signal can reach it. Located in the district of Bragança, it preserves the charm of the most authentic villages in Trás-os-Montes.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to hike the Deer Trail (PR12 Guadramil)?
faqs.answer: This moderate trail is approximately 8.8 km (5.5 miles) long and generally takes around 2 hours and 29 minutes to 3 hours to complete, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Deer Trail (PR12 Guadramil)?
faqs.answer: The trail primarily consists of hard-packed dirt and rocks, offering easily accessible paths. You'll experience a varied elevation profile with a cumulative climb of about 184 meters (604 feet) and a similar descent.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail is known as the 'Deer Route' for a reason! You have a good chance of spotting Red Deer (Cervus elaphus) and European Roe Deer (Capreolus capreolus), especially during their mating season from September to November. The wild landscape of Montesinho Natural Park also hosts other local fauna.

faqs.question: Where does the Deer Trail (PR12 Guadramil) start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail is circular and begins and ends in the charming Guadramil Village. Parking for hikers is typically available within or near the village.

faqs.question: Is the Deer Trail (PR12 Guadramil) dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Deer Trail (PR12 Guadramil) is generally dog-friendly. However, as it passes through Montesinho Natural Park and is known for its wildlife, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash to protect both your pet and the local fauna.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The Deer Trail (PR12 Guadramil) can be hiked all year round. For wildlife enthusiasts, the mating season for deer (September to November) offers the best chance to see them. It's advisable to avoid hiking on particularly foggy days for safety and visibility.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for hiking in Montesinho Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for hiking the Deer Trail (PR12 Guadramil) within Montesinho Natural Park. However, always respect park regulations and leave no trace.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest or scenic views along the Deer Trail (PR12 Guadramil)?
faqs.answer: You'll enjoy a View of the Rolling Hills in Montesinho Natural Park, traverse a Road Along the Guadramil River, and explore the picturesque Guadramil Village with its distinctive schist houses. The trail also offers tranquil, open landscapes throughout.

faqs.question: Is the Deer Trail (PR12 Guadramil) suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Classified as moderate, the trail requires good fitness but is suitable for most skill levels due to its easily accessible paths. Families with some hiking experience should find it enjoyable, but consider the 3-hour duration and varied terrain.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or places to eat in Guadramil village?
faqs.answer: Yes, Guadramil Village, where the trail starts and ends, is a small settlement. While specific amenities like cafes or restaurants might be limited, it's a good idea to check local listings or bring your own provisions, especially during off-peak seasons.

faqs.question: How can I get to Guadramil village and the trailhead by public transport?
faqs.answer: Public transport options to remote villages like Guadramil in Montesinho Natural Park can be limited. It's recommended to check regional bus schedules from larger towns like Bragança, or consider driving to the village for easier access to the trailhead.

waypoints.name: View of the Rolling Hills in Montesinho Natural Park
